title: Cross-Attention
description:
Implement Cross-Attention as used in the Transformer decoder.

In cross-attention:
- Queries (Q) come from the decoder's current hidden states
- Keys (K) and Values (V) come from the encoder's output

This allows each decoder position to attend to all encoder positions.

Formula:
```
CrossAttention(Q_dec, K_enc, V_enc) = softmax(Q_dec @ K_enc.T / sqrt(d_k)) @ V_enc
```

With learnable projections W_Q, W_K, W_V, W_O (multi-head version).

Implement `cross_attention(Q_dec, K_enc, V_enc, W_Q, W_K, W_V, W_O, num_heads)` that projects and performs multi-head cross-attention.
constraints: No causal mask (decoder attends to all encoder positions)
starter_code:
import numpy as np

def cross_attention(Q_dec, K_enc, V_enc, W_Q, W_K, W_V, W_O, num_heads):
    """Multi-head cross-attention.

    Args:
        Q_dec: Decoder queries (seq_dec, d_model)
        K_enc: Encoder keys (seq_enc, d_model)
        V_enc: Encoder values (seq_enc, d_model)
        W_Q: Query projection (d_model, d_model)
        W_K: Key projection (d_model, d_model)
        W_V: Value projection (d_model, d_model)
        W_O: Output projection (d_model, d_model)
        num_heads: Number of attention heads

    Returns:
        output: (seq_dec, d_model)
    """
    pass
solution_code:
import numpy as np

def cross_attention(Q_dec, K_enc, V_enc, W_Q, W_K, W_V, W_O, num_heads):
    seq_dec, d_model = Q_dec.shape
    seq_enc = K_enc.shape[0]
    d_k = d_model // num_heads

    # Project
    Q = Q_dec @ W_Q.T  # (seq_dec, d_model)
    K = K_enc @ W_K.T  # (seq_enc, d_model)
    V = V_enc @ W_V.T  # (seq_enc, d_model)

    # Split into heads
    Q_h = Q.reshape(seq_dec, num_heads, d_k).transpose(1, 0, 2)  # (H, seq_dec, d_k)
    K_h = K.reshape(seq_enc, num_heads, d_k).transpose(1, 0, 2)  # (H, seq_enc, d_k)
    V_h = V.reshape(seq_enc, num_heads, d_k).transpose(1, 0, 2)  # (H, seq_enc, d_k)

    # Attention scores: (H, seq_dec, seq_enc)
    scores = Q_h @ K_h.transpose(0, 2, 1) / np.sqrt(d_k)
    scores_max = scores.max(axis=-1, keepdims=True)
    exp_scores = np.exp(scores - scores_max)
    weights = exp_scores / exp_scores.sum(axis=-1, keepdims=True)

    # Attended values: (H, seq_dec, d_k)
    head_out = weights @ V_h

    # Concat and project
    concat = head_out.transpose(1, 0, 2).reshape(seq_dec, d_model)
    return concat @ W_O.T
explanation:
Cross-attention connects the encoder and decoder in sequence-to-sequence Transformers (e.g., for translation).

Key distinction from self-attention:
- Self-attention: Q, K, V all come from the same sequence
- Cross-attention: Q comes from the decoder, K and V come from the encoder

This means the decoder sequence length (seq_dec) can differ from the encoder sequence length (seq_enc). The attention weight matrix has shape (seq_dec, seq_enc), and each decoder position produces a weighted combination of all encoder positions.

No causal mask is needed here because the encoder output is fully computed and available — the decoder should see all encoder positions.

test_cases.name: test_output_shape
test_cases.code:
import numpy as np
np.random.seed(0)
seq_dec, seq_enc, d_model, num_heads = 4, 10, 32, 4
Q_dec = np.random.randn(seq_dec, d_model)
K_enc = np.random.randn(seq_enc, d_model)
V_enc = np.random.randn(seq_enc, d_model)
W_Q = np.random.randn(d_model, d_model) * 0.1
W_K = np.random.randn(d_model, d_model) * 0.1
W_V = np.random.randn(d_model, d_model) * 0.1
W_O = np.random.randn(d_model, d_model) * 0.1
out = cross_attention(Q_dec, K_enc, V_enc, W_Q, W_K, W_V, W_O, num_heads)
assert out.shape == (seq_dec, d_model), f'Expected ({seq_dec}, {d_model}), got {out.shape}'

test_cases.name: test_same_seq_len
test_cases.code:
import numpy as np
np.random.seed(1)
seq, d_model, num_heads = 6, 16, 2
Q = np.random.randn(seq, d_model)
K = np.random.randn(seq, d_model)
V = np.random.randn(seq, d_model)
W_Q = np.eye(d_model); W_K = np.eye(d_model); W_V = np.eye(d_model); W_O = np.eye(d_model)
out = cross_attention(Q, K, V, W_Q, W_K, W_V, W_O, num_heads)
assert out.shape == (seq, d_model)

test_cases.name: test_decoder_longer_than_encoder
test_cases.code:
import numpy as np
np.random.seed(2)
seq_dec, seq_enc, d_model, num_heads = 8, 3, 16, 2
Q = np.random.randn(seq_dec, d_model)
K = np.random.randn(seq_enc, d_model)
V = np.random.randn(seq_enc, d_model)
W_Q = np.random.randn(d_model, d_model) * 0.1
W_K = np.random.randn(d_model, d_model) * 0.1
W_V = np.random.randn(d_model, d_model) * 0.1
W_O = np.random.randn(d_model, d_model) * 0.1
out = cross_attention(Q, K, V, W_Q, W_K, W_V, W_O, num_heads)
assert out.shape == (seq_dec, d_model)

test_cases.name: test_encoder_content_affects_output
test_cases.code:
import numpy as np
np.random.seed(3)
seq_dec, seq_enc, d_model, num_heads = 3, 5, 16, 2
Q = np.random.randn(seq_dec, d_model)
K1 = np.random.randn(seq_enc, d_model)
K2 = np.random.randn(seq_enc, d_model)
V = np.random.randn(seq_enc, d_model)
W_Q = np.random.randn(d_model, d_model) * 0.1
W_K = np.random.randn(d_model, d_model) * 0.1
W_V = np.random.randn(d_model, d_model) * 0.1
W_O = np.random.randn(d_model, d_model) * 0.1
out1 = cross_attention(Q, K1, V, W_Q, W_K, W_V, W_O, num_heads)
out2 = cross_attention(Q, K2, V, W_Q, W_K, W_V, W_O, num_heads)
assert not np.allclose(out1, out2), 'Different K should yield different outputs'

test_cases.name: test_zero_weights_zero_output
test_cases.code:
import numpy as np
seq_dec, seq_enc, d_model, num_heads = 4, 6, 8, 2
Q = np.random.randn(seq_dec, d_model)
K = np.random.randn(seq_enc, d_model)
V = np.random.randn(seq_enc, d_model)
out = cross_attention(Q, K, V,
    np.zeros((d_model, d_model)), np.zeros((d_model, d_model)),
    np.zeros((d_model, d_model)), np.zeros((d_model, d_model)), num_heads)
assert np.allclose(out, 0)

hints: Same implementation as MHA but Q comes from decoder (seq_dec) and K, V from encoder (seq_enc)
Score matrix shape: (H, seq_dec, seq_enc) — each decoder position attends to all encoder positions
No mask needed: decoder attends to all encoder positions freely
